archetypes.name: CLI Command
archetypes.description: CLI command handlers and subcommand implementations.

analysisHints.description: CLI command modules handle user-facing terminal commands

archetypes.name: Component
archetypes.description: React/UI component files with JSX-returning functions.

analysisHints.description: Components have inflated call counts from hooks, state setters, and JSX rendering
High fan-in expected — config modules are widely imported

archetypes.name: Entry Point
archetypes.description: Module entry points, public APIs, and CLI entry files where uncalled exports are expected.

analysisHints.description: Uncalled exports are expected in entry points

archetypes.name: Gateway
archetypes.description: Re-export-heavy gateway modules that concentrate cross-package imports.

analysisHints.description: Gateway modules are expected to re-export heavily

archetypes.name: Hook
archetypes.description: React hooks — custom hooks encapsulating reusable stateful logic.

analysisHints.description: React hooks follow the use* naming convention

archetypes.name: Model
archetypes.description: Data models, schemas, and ORM definitions.

archetypes.name: Page
archetypes.description: Page-level components, views, or screens — top-level UI entry points for routes.

analysisHints.description: Page/view components orchestrate many sub-components and have inflated call counts

archetypes.name: Route Handler
archetypes.description: Server-side HTTP route handlers (Express, Hono, Koa, etc.).

archetypes.name: Route Module
archetypes.description: Framework convention route modules (Remix/React Router) with loader/action/default exports.

analysisHints.description: Convention exports are expected in route modules

archetypes.name: Schema
archetypes.description: Runtime validation schemas and data shape definitions (Zod, Yup, Joi, etc.).

analysisHints.description: Schema modules define runtime data shapes for validation

archetypes.name: Service
archetypes.description: Service layer modules — API clients, data fetching, and business logic orchestration.

analysisHints.description: Service modules orchestrate business logic and external calls

archetypes.name: Store
archetypes.description: State management stores and slices where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for state management modules

archetypes.name: Types & Constants
archetypes.description: Type definitions, constants, and enums — companion files, not logic.

analysisHints.description: Unidirectional coupling and high fan-in expected for type files

archetypes.name: Utility
archetypes.description: Shared utility, helper, and infrastructure modules where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for utility modules

evidence.detail: Root-level CI pipeline orchestration script — executes analysis and PRD health validation as a standalone entry point.
Root-level help command handler for the CLI.
Business logic for adaptive agent analysis — determines behavioral adjustments based on agent state.
Review analysis logic — evaluates agent output or task completion quality.
Spin detection service — identifies when the agent is in a non-productive loop.
Stuck detection service — identifies when the agent is blocked or not making progress.
Summary generation service — produces structured summaries of agent runs or task state.
Workflow analysis service — evaluates agent workflow state and transitions.
Handles agent completion logic — determines when and how an agent run finishes.
CLI-mode agent loop — drives the agent turn cycle when invoked from the CLI.
Heartbeat service — maintains liveness signals during long-running agent loops.
Core agent loop — orchestrates turn iteration and tool dispatch.
Shared utilities used across lifecycle modules — no standalone behavior.
Display helpers for task state — formats and renders task progress to the terminal.
Token budget management — tracks and enforces token limits across agent turns.
Token usage tracking — records and aggregates token consumption per run.
Brief generation service — builds the task brief fed to the agent at the start of a run.
Prompt construction service — assembles the system/user prompts for the LLM.
Tool schema definitions for the agent — defines available tool signatures without containing orchestration logic.
CLI help command implementation for the hench package.
Guard command logic — evaluates or intercepts commands for safety/policy enforcement.
Type contracts for the guard system — defines interfaces and enums, no runtime logic.
Path-related guard utilities — resolves or validates filesystem paths for the guard layer.
Guard policy enforcement — applies rules to determine whether an action is permitted.
PRD operation service — wraps rex PRD mutations and queries used by the agent.
Explicitly documented as a gateway in CLAUDE.md — concentrates all cross-package imports from rex into a single re-export module.
Metrics utility for tracking concurrent process execution — no domain logic.
Shell execution utility — wraps child_process for running shell commands.
General execution utility — core process spawning and management primitives.
Process lifecycle management — handles start, monitor, and teardown of child processes.
Concurrency/rate limiter — infrastructure module used across the process subsystem
Monitors memory usage — shared infrastructure utility
Throttles execution based on memory pressure — infrastructure helper
Process pool management — orchestrates lifecycle of worker processes
Manages execution queue — orchestrates task scheduling and dispatch
Formats queue output — pure formatting helper
Priority-based scheduling logic — orchestrates task ordering
Glob matching helper — shared utility across hench
Tool interface/type contracts — type definitions without logic
Dispatches tool calls — orchestrates routing to concrete tool implementations
Shell execution primitive — low-level infrastructure used by multiple tools
File operation tools — shared utility with likely high fan-in
Git operation tools — shared utility helpers
Rex integration tool — API client and business logic for PRD operations
Orchestrates test execution — business logic for running and interpreting tests
Task completion validation logic — utility for checking done criteria
API-based LLM provider — API client implementation
Authentication helpers — shared utility for credential resolution
CLI-based LLM provider — wraps CLI invocation as a service
Codex CLI provider — vendor-specific service implementation
Factory for creating LLM clients — infrastructure utility
Execution primitive for LLM calls — low-level helper
Core LLM client — main service layer for LLM interactions
LLM configuration module — settings and defaults for the client
LLM type definitions — interfaces, enums, and constants
Project-level configuration — reads and manages project config settings
Exports project directory name constants — static configuration with high fan-in, no logic.
Pure type definitions and interfaces (LLMProvider, ProviderCapability, StreamChunk) — no runtime logic.
ProviderRegistry class implementing a registry/factory pattern with a default singleton — shared foundation with high fan-in.
ProviderSession wraps the registry for stateful vendor switching; factory function createProviderSession() — infrastructure utility.
Pure helper functions (editDistance, suggestCommands, formatTypoSuggestion) for CLI typo correction — no side effects or dependencies.
Pure parsing functions for token usage across SDK/CLI formats — stateless, no I/O.
Hash-based acknowledgment store with pure functions (computeFindingHash, isAcknowledged) plus simple file I/O persistence.
Orchestrates an LLM call to consolidate oversized proposals — prompt building, response parsing, decision logic.
LoE-threshold-driven recursive task decomposition pipeline with LLM orchestration and Zod schema validation.
Multi-signal similarity scoring with union-find clustering to deduplicate proposals — complex domain logic.
Pure function that renders a diff view comparing proposals against the existing PRD — no I/O or side effects.
Structured requirements extraction engine with multi-strategy parsing (markdown/JSON/YAML) and LLM disambiguation fallback.
Pure file format validation — magic byte detection, size checks, format-specific syntax warnings — no external calls.
Interactive spec builder with LLM clarification rounds and readline prompts — stateful orchestration pipeline.
LLM-powered proposal modification pipeline with prompt building, response parsing, and quality validation.
Converts scan results into hierarchical proposal structure with multi-strategy task placement and priority sorting.
Core LLM reasoning module for proposal generation — shared prompt templates, token tracking, heavy analysis lifting.
Compares scan results against existing PRD using fuzzy matching and update candidate detection.
LLM-powered PRD reshape proposals (merge/split/reparent/update) with Zod validation and three operation modes.
Multi-source scanning (tests, docs, sourcevision, package.json) with file globbing and multi-format extraction.
Pure intent classification and vague-phrase detection for natural-language modification requests — no I/O.
Static COMMAND_DEFS record mapping command names to HelpDefinition metadata — constants with no runtime logic.
Implements MCP tool handlers orchestrating store operations, validation, and logging across multiple subsystems.
Creates and configures the Rex MCP server — tool registration, resource setup, transport abstraction.
Pure CLI flag parsing and validation helpers (level, format, integer parsing) with user-friendly error messages.
Detects conflicts between recommendations and existing PRD using blended multi-signal similarity scoring.
Atomic PRD item creation from recommendations with placement validation, conflict detection, and DAG validation.
Exports a single static string constant containing workflow instructions — pure data, no logic.
Built-in archetype definitions as static configuration objects with signals, hints, and a lookup map builder.
Pure heuristic functions (isBreakingChange, inferSignificance, classifyItem) using pattern matching — no I/O or external calls.
Collects and orchestrates branch work data — service layer with business logic
Filters/transforms branch work data — pure transformation logic
Persistent JSON storage for branch work records with read/write and validation — named and structured as a data store
Produces analysis findings from call graph data — business logic output layer
Call graph construction and analysis — core analysis service
API client wrapper for Claude — service layer for external API calls
Reads and parses LLM completion responses — infrastructure utility
React component catalog analysis — analysis service orchestrating component discovery
CONTEXT.md generation — output service orchestrating context document creation
Batch AI enrichment orchestration — coordinates multiple enrichment calls
Constants and helpers for enrichment configuration — named config module with tuning constants
Parses AI enrichment responses — pure parsing utility
Per-zone AI enrichment pass — orchestrates enrichment for individual zones
Main enrichment entry point — orchestrates the full enrichment pipeline
Import graph analysis — core analysis service for dependency detection
File inventory collection — core analysis service for file discovery
Generates llms.txt output file — output service
Louvain community detection algorithm implementation — pure graph algorithm utility
Manifest read/write management — service layer for persisting analysis metadata
Next steps recommendation analysis — analysis service producing actionable output
Risk scoring algorithm — pure scoring logic, likely shared across analyzers
Detects server-side routes from source files — analysis service
Token usage tracking and accounting — shared utility for LLM cost tracking
Aggregates workspace-level analysis data — service orchestrating cross-zone aggregation
Detects and analyzes zone crossings in workspace — analysis service
Workspace-level analysis coordination — top-level analysis orchestrator
Generates per-zone output files — output service for zone-specific artifacts
Zone detection and management — core analysis service using Louvain
CLI help content definitions — shared utility consumed by CLI command handlers
CLI command handler that implements the `sourcevision mcp` subcommand, registering MCP tools and starting the server transport
CLI command stub implementing `sourcevision serve`, delegating to the web package CLI
Service module that orchestrates PDF generation from analysis data using PDFKit — business logic, no I/O at module level
Pure transformation functions (no I/O, no side effects) for converting BranchWorkRecord into PR markdown
Shared utility for merging chunked inventory/imports data — high fan-in expected across analyzers
Canonical deterministic sorting utilities for all schema output types — high fan-in across the package
esbuild configuration and build orchestration script for the web package assets
Development server orchestration script — spawns build watchers and the dev server process
Landing page JS entry point — top-level side-effect script wiring DOM interactions (theme toggle, clipboard, scroll animations)
Cache service with filesystem-based invalidation and memory bounding — stateful business logic for aggregation results
Stateful metrics collection service with ring-buffer storage and snapshot API for dashboard consumption
Single re-export gateway concentrating all web→sourcevision runtime imports into one explicit surface
Stateful incremental aggregator service — maintains in-memory cache with mtime-based change detection
Deprecated gateway that re-exports from rex-gateway and domain-gateway — pure re-export, no logic
Port availability detection and dynamic allocation utilities — shared infrastructure with no state
Pure type and enum definitions for PR markdown refresh diagnostic codes and stages — no logic
Stateful per-process RSS tracking service with ring buffers, high-memory warnings, and snapshot API
Single re-export gateway concentrating all web→rex runtime imports — explicit cross-package surface
Lazy-built, auto-invalidating in-memory search index with scoring logic — stateful service for PRD search
Server entry point that creates the HTTP server and wires all route handlers, watchers, and WebSocket manager
Periodic scheduler service that cross-references usage aggregation with PRD state and broadcasts cleanup events
WebSocket manager service implementing RFC 6455 protocol, broadcast, and keepalive — stateful connection management
Singleton metrics accumulator tracking WebSocket connection lifecycle, cleanup events, and broadcast statistics
Pure constants — data file name mappings shared between server and viewer layers, no logic
Application bootstrap entry point — one-time startup side effects (theme, polling, visibility monitoring) called before first render
Self-contained Barnes-Hut physics engine for force-directed layout — pure computation, no framework dependencies
Imperative SVG rendering engine extracted from a view — DOM manipulation utility class with no framework coupling
Data fetching and validation service with lazy loading, selective refresh, and polling registration for the viewer
Standalone rate-limiting utility with queue deduplication — zero framework dependencies, reusable infrastructure
Infrastructure helper that merges/combines messages in the viewer messaging pipeline
Infrastructure helper that throttles message flow in the viewer messaging pipeline
Infrastructure helper that deduplicates in-flight requests
Infrastructure module that detects and reports viewer crashes
Infrastructure module that monitors DOM rendering performance
Infrastructure gate that controls when DOM updates are allowed to proceed
Infrastructure module managing feature fallback under resource pressure
Infrastructure module tracking memory usage in the viewer
Infrastructure helper throttling viewer refresh cycles
Infrastructure gate that buffers and controls response processing
Infrastructure helper that batches DOM/state updates for efficiency
Infrastructure helper dispatching batched polling ticks
Orchestrates the full polling lifecycle — coordinates timers, visibility, and restart logic
Infrastructure helper handling polling restart logic
Shared state container for the polling subsystem
Infrastructure helper abstracting Page Visibility API for polling
Infrastructure helper managing polling interval timer
Infrastructure gate suppressing ticks when the tab is hidden
Compatibility shim adapting between schema versions
Color constant definitions for the visualization layer — pure data, no logic
Helper functions for building/transforming flow graph data
Metric computation helpers for the visualization layer
Root-level CI script for PR validation checks
Root-level script for refreshing build/analysis artifacts
Root-level script for regenerating the PRD plan
Root-level script for running validation after refresh
Server orchestration entry point — starts dashboard and MCP endpoints (per CLAUDE.md)
